countries.name: Afghanistan
countries.climate_summary: Perched at the crossroads of Central and South Asia, Afghanistan boasts a predominantly arid to semi-arid climate, with scorching summers and cool winters. The region experiences its peak heat from June through August, when temperatures soar above 30°C (86°F) and rainfall is minimal, making it an ideal season for relocation or a visit if seeking warm weather and dry conditions. For those desiring mild temperatures and a break from scorching spells, the spring months of April to May offer a refreshing reprieve with average highs around 24°C (75°F) and slightly fewer rainfall days.

The peak of warmth and rainfall comes during July and August, when maximum temperatures soar to over 27°C, accompanied by more than two rainy days per week; yet, this is also considered the transition period into hurricane season. For those seeking the most comfortable weather, May through June offers ideal conditions with lower humidity, slightly cooler averages around 26°C, and fewer rain showers, making it an optimal time for relocation or a visit.
